skill.name: project-interview
skill.description: Guides conversational interviews that capture learner goals, background, preferences, and tone for personalized learning project profiles.
skill.summary: Conduct learner interviews and convert responses into confirmed profile notes for project setup.
skill.author: BitYoungjae

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography, reconnaissance, obfuscation, and command execution patterns, but review found these were false positives from markdown prose, examples, and fenced code blocks. No prompt injection, network access, credential handling, or executable command behavior was found. The only confirmed risk factor is legitimate filesystem writing for persona.md and interview-data.md outputs.

low_findings.title: Legitimate Filesystem Write Capability
low_findings.description: The skill declares Write access and instructs the agent to create persona.md and interview-data.md. This is expected for the skill purpose, but users should understand it writes local project files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Write tool is explicitly declared and the output section names files to create. The behavior is transparent and scoped to project interview artifacts.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static findings at the reported lines are ordinary skill descriptions and learner interview examples. No cryptographic implementation, hashing, encryption, or weak algorithm usage was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual line review shows natural-language documentation and examples only. There is no code path or crypto primitive at these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reported lines mention learner context, examples, and interview anti-patterns. References such as Linux or Ubuntu describe a learner environment and do not collect host system data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is conversational guidance and does not request commands, host enumeration, environment variables, or sensitive system details.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fence Detected as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The backtick findings are markdown template fences and inline filenames. They define interview-data.md formatting and do not execute Ruby, shell, or other commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines are markdown code fences or inline code spans. There is no executable language block or command invocation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: High Entropy Content
low_findings.description: The entropy heuristic appears to be triggered by multilingual Korean and English interview examples. The file is readable markdown and not binary, encrypted, or obfuscated.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows plain markdown with visible conversation examples. No encoded payloads, compressed data, or hidden directives were found.

content.value_statement: Personalized learning projects need clear goals, level, motivation, and format preferences. This skill guides a natural interview and records confirmed profile details for project setup.
actual_capabilities: Guides open-ended interviews about learning goals, motivation, current level, and preferred document style.
Provides conversation examples for rich, minimal, ambiguous, and emotionally nuanced learner inputs.
Shows how to summarize a learner profile conversationally before final confirmation.
Defines an interview-data.md template for conversation records, profile summaries, and tone notes.
Supports Korean and English interview examples for multilingual learner onboarding.
limitations: It provides guidance and templates, not an autonomous interview application.
It depends on the calling agent to ask questions and write final files correctly.
It does not validate learner claims or assess skill level with tests.
It is focused on learning project setup, not general hiring or clinical interviews.

use_cases.title: Set up a custom learning project
use_cases.description: Capture a learner topic, background, goals, and preferred content depth before generating project files.

use_cases.title: Prepare a student profile for course design
use_cases.description: Convert a short conversation into structured notes that help tailor examples, tone, and lesson scope.

use_cases.title: Standardize onboarding for learning assistants
use_cases.description: Use consistent interview patterns so AI assistants gather enough context without sounding like a rigid form.

prompt_templates.title: Start a learner interview
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the project-interview skill to ask me natural questions about what I want to learn and why. Summarize my profile before writing anything.

prompt_templates.title: Turn brief answers into a profile
prompt_templates.prompt: I only have a rough topic. Use the interview examples to ask follow-up questions until you know my level, motivation, style, and desired scope.

prompt_templates.title: Record confirmed interview data
prompt_templates.prompt: After the interview, create interview-data.md using the template. Include the conversation summary, key insights, and language or tone notes.

prompt_templates.title: Handle ambiguous learning goals
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the conversation-flow examples to clarify an ambiguous learning goal. Identify the likely domain, emotional cues, constraints, and profile assumptions before asking for confirmation.

output_examples.input: A learner says they want to learn React for job preparation and know basic HTML and CSS.
output_examples.output: The assistant asks about JavaScript comfort, career goal, backend interest, and preferred learning depth, then confirms a beginner front-end profile.

output_examples.input: A learner wants Rust async programming for backend work and already understands ownership basics.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies an intermediate technology profile, practical motivation, Linux environment, and a medium-length project focused on async patterns.

output_examples.input: A learner gives an unclear topic and expresses fear or uncertainty.
output_examples.output: The assistant clarifies the domain, acknowledges the emotional cue, and proposes a gentle learning scope before requesting confirmation.

best_practices: Ask open questions first, then follow details the learner already provided.
Confirm the profile summary with the learner before creating project files.
Preserve the learner language, tone, and repeated terms in interview notes.
anti_patterns: Do not ask fixed survey questions when the answer is already clear.
Do not infer sensitive personal details that the learner did not volunteer.
Do not write final profile files before the learner confirms the summary.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It helps create persona.md, interview-data.md, and project metadata for a learning project setup.

faq.question: Does it run commands or call external services?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed files contain markdown guidance and templates only.

faq.question: Can it interview in Korean?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples include Korean conversations and notes about formality and tone.

faq.question: Is it only for programming topics?
faq.answer: No. The conversation examples cover technology, arts, ambiguous topics, and general learning goals.

faq.question: Does it assess learner skill automatically?
faq.answer: No. It infers level from conversation and should confirm the summary with the learner.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk low?
faq.answer: The skill can write local profile files, but the reviewed content does not show malicious behavior.
